title: Discrete Mathematics - Set Theory and Algebra - Group Theory

questions.question:
Show that if \( G \) is a group such that \( (a* b)^2 = a^2*b^2 \) for all \( a, b \) belonging to \( G \), then \( G \) is an abelian.


GATE CSE 1988 | Question: 2xviii

Match the pairs in the following questions:\[ \begin{array}{|ll|ll|}\hline (a) & \text{Groups} & (p) & \text{Associativity} \\\hline  (b) & \text{Semigroups} & (q) & \text{Identity} \\\hline   (c) & \text{Monoids} & (r)  & \text{Commutativity} \\\hline (d) & \text{Abelian groups} & (s)  & \text{Left inverse}  \\\hline \end{array} \]


GATE CSE 1990 | Question: 2-x

If \( G \) is a group of even order, then show that there exists an element \( a≠e \), the identity in \( G \), such that \( a^2 = e \).


GATE CSE 1992 | Question: 14a

Let \( \left(\{ p,q \},*\right) \) be a semigroup where \( p*p=q \). Show that:

  1. \( p*q=q*p \) and
  2. \( q*q=q \)


GATE CSE 1993 | Question: 28

Some group \( (G, o) \) is known to be abelian. Then, which one of the following is true for \( G \)?



GATE CSE 1994 | Question: 1.10
options: \( (goh)^2 = g^2oh^2 \text{ for every } g, h \in G \)
questions.correct_answer: \( (goh)^2 = g^2oh^2 \text{ for every } g, h \in G \)

questions.question:
Let \( A \) be the set of all non-singular matrices over real number and let \( * \) be the matrix multiplication operation. Then



GATE CSE 1995 | Question: 2.17
options: \( A \) is closed under \( * \) but \( \langle A, *\rangle \) is not a semigroup.
\( \langle A, *\rangle \) is a semigroup but not a monoid.
\( \langle A, * \rangle \) is a monoid but not a group.
\( \langle A, *\rangle \) is a a group but not an abelian group.
questions.correct_answer: \( \langle A, *\rangle \) is a a group but not an abelian group.

questions.question:
Let \( G_1 \) and \( G_2 \) be subgroups of a group \( G \).



GATE CSE 1995 | Question: 21
options: Show that \( G_1 \cap G_2 \) is also a subgroup of \( G \).
Is \( G_1 \cup G_2 \) always a subgroup of \( G \)?.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements is FALSE?



GATE CSE 1996 | Question: 1.4
options: The set of rational numbers is an abelian group under addition
The set of integers in an abelian group under addition
The set of rational numbers form an abelian group under multiplication
The set of real numbers excluding zero is an abelian group under multiplication
questions.correct_answer: The set of rational numbers form an abelian group under multiplication

questions.question:
Which one of the following is false?



GATE CSE 1996 | Question: 2.4
options: The set of all bijective functions on a finite set forms a group under function composition
The set \( \{1, 2, \dots p-1\} \) forms a group under multiplication mod \( p \), where \( p \) is a prime number
The set of all strings over a finite alphabet forms a group under concatenation
A subset \( S \neq \emptyset \) of \( G \) is a subgroup of the group \( \langle G, * \rangle \) if and only if for any pair of elements \( a, b \in S, a * b^{-1} \in S \)
questions.correct_answer: The set of all strings over a finite alphabet forms a group under concatenation

questions.question:
Let \( \left(Z, *\right) \) be an algebraic structure where \( Z \) is the set of integers and the operation \( * \) is defined by \( n*m = \max(n,m) \). Which of the following statements is true for \( \left(Z, *\right) \)?



GATE CSE 1997 | Question: 3.1

Let \( (A, *) \) be a semigroup, Furthermore, for every \( a \) and \( b \) in \( A \), if \( a \neq b \), then \( a*b \neq b*a \).

  1. Show that for every \( a \) in \( A \), \( a*a=a \)
  2. Show that for every \( a \), \( b \) in \( A \), \( a*b*a=a \)
  3. Show that for every \( a,b,c \) in \( A \), \( a*b*c=a*c \)


GATE CSE 1998 | Question: 12

Let \( G \) be a finite group and \( H \) be a subgroup of \( G \). For \( a \in G \), define \( aH=\left\{ah \mid h \in H\right\} \).

  1. Show that \( |aH| = |bH|. \)

  2. Show that for every pair of elements \( a, b \in G \), either \( aH = bH \) or \( aH \) and \( bH \) are disjoint.

  3. Use the above to argue that the order of \( H \) must divide the order of \( G. \)



GATE CSE 1999 | Question: 4

Let \( S= \{0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7\} \) and \( ⊗ \) denote multiplication modulo \( 8, \) that is, \( x ⊗ y= (xy) \mod 8 \)

  1. Prove that \( ( \{ 0, 1\}, ⊗) \) is not a group.
  2. Write three distinct groups \( (G, ⊗) \) where \( G ⊂ S \) and \( G \) has \( 2 \) elements.


GATE CSE 2000 | Question: 4

Which of the following is true?



GATE CSE 2002 | Question: 1.6
options: The set of all rational negative numbers forms a group under multiplication.
The set of all non-singular matrices forms a group under multiplication.
The set of all matrices forms a group under multiplication.
questions.correct_answer: The set of all non-singular matrices forms a group under multiplication.

questions.question:
Consider the set \( \Sigma^* \) of all strings over the alphabet \( \Sigma = \{0, 1\} \). \( \Sigma^* \) with the concatenation operator for strings



GATE CSE 2003 | Question: 7
options: does not have a right identity element
forms a group if the empty string is removed from \( \Sigma^* \)

questions.question:
The following is the incomplete operation table of a \( 4- \)element group.

\[ \begin{array}{|l|l|l|l|l|} \hline \textbf{*} & \textbf{e}& \textbf{a} &\textbf{b} & \textbf{c}\\\hline \textbf{e} & \text{e}& \text{a} & \text{b} & \text{c} \\\hline \textbf{a} & \text{a}& \text{b} & \text{c} & \text{e}\\\hline \textbf{b}\\\hline \textbf{c} \\\hline\end{array} \]

The last row of the table is



GATE CSE 2004 | Question: 72

The set \(\{1, 2, 4, 7, 8, 11, 13, 14\}\) is a group under multiplication modulo \( 15 \). The inverses of \( 4 \) and \( 7 \) are respectively:



GATE CSE 2005 | Question: 13

Consider the set \( H \) of all \( 3 * 3 \) matrices of the type \[ \left( \begin{array}{ccc} a & f & e \\ 0 & b & d \\ 0 & 0 & c \end{array} \right) \] where \( a,b,c,d,e \) and \( f \) are real numbers and \( abc ≠ 0. \) Under the matrix multiplication operation, the set \( H \) is:



GATE CSE 2005 | Question: 46
options: a semi group but not a monoid
neither a group nor a semi group

questions.question:
The set \( \{1,2,3,5,7,8,9\} \) under multiplication modulo \( 10 \) is not a group. Given below are four possible reasons. Which one of them is false?



GATE CSE 2006 | Question: 3

How many different non-isomorphic Abelian groups of order \( 4 \) are there?



GATE CSE 2007 | Question: 21

Which one of the following is NOT necessarily a property of a Group?



GATE CSE 2009 | Question: 1

For the composition table of a cyclic group shown below:
\[ \begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|c|} \hline \textbf{*} & \textbf{a}& \textbf{b} &\textbf{c} & \textbf{d}\\\hline \textbf{a} & \text{a}& \text{b} & \text{c} & \text{d} \\\hline \textbf{b} & \text{b}& \text{a} & \text{d} &\text{c}\\\hline \textbf{c} & \text{c}& \text{d} & \text{b} & \text{a}\\\hline \textbf{d} & \text{d}& \text{c} & \text{a} & \text{b} \\\hline \end{array} \]
Which one of the following choices is correct?



GATE CSE 2009 | Question: 22

Consider the set \( S = \{1, ω, ω^2\} \), where \( ω \) and \( ω^2 \) are cube roots of unity. If \( * \) denotes the multiplication operation, the structure \( (S, *) \) forms



GATE CSE 2010 | Question: 4

Let \( G \) be a group with \( 15 \) elements. Let \( L \) be a subgroup of \( G \). It is known that \( L \neq\ G \) and that the size of \( L \) is at least \( 4 \). The size of \( L \) is __________.


GATE CSE 2014 Set 3 | Question: 3

There are two elements \( x,\:y \) in a group \( (G,*) \) such that every element in the group can be written as a product of some number of \( x \)'s and \( y \)'s in some order. It is known that
\[ x*x=y*y=x*y*x*y=y*x*y*x=e \]
where \( e \) is the identity element. The maximum number of elements in such a group is ____.


GATE CSE 2014 Set 3 | Question: 50

Let \( G \) be a finite group on \( 84 \) elements. The size of a largest possible proper subgroup of \( G \) is _____


GATE CSE 2018 | Question: 19

Let \( G \) be an arbitrary group. Consider the following relations on \( G \):

  • \( R_1: \forall a , b \in G, \: a R_1 b \text{ if and only if } \exists g \in G \text{ such that } a = g^{-1}bg \)
  • \( R_2: \forall a , b \in G, \: a R_2 b \text{ if and only if } a= b^{-1} \)

Which of the above is/are equivalence relation/relations?



GATE CSE 2019 | Question: 10

Let \( G \) be a group of \( 35 \) elements. Then the largest possible size of a subgroup of \( G \) other than \( G \) itself is _______.


GATE CSE 2020 | Question: 18

Let \( G \) be a group of order \( 6 \), and \( H \) be a subgroup of \( G \) such that \( 1<|H|<6 \). Which one of the following options is correct?



GATE CSE 2021 Set 1 | Question: 34
options: Both \( G \) and \( H \) are always cyclic
\( G \) may not be cyclic, but \( H \) is always cyclic
\( G \) is always cyclic, but \( H \) may not be cyclic
Both \( G \) and \( H \) may not be cyclic
questions.correct_answer: \( G \) may not be cyclic, but \( H \) is always cyclic

questions.question:
Which of the following statements is/are \( \text{TRUE} \) for a group \( \textit{G}? \)



GATE CSE 2022 | Question: 17
options: If for all \( x,y \in \textit{G}, \; (xy)^{2} = x^{2} y^{2}, \) then \( \textit{G} \) is commutative.
If for all \( x \in \textit{G}, \; x^{2} = 1, \) then \( \textit{G} \) is commutative. Here, \( 1 \) is the identity element of \( \textit{G}. \)
If the order of \( \textit{G} \) is \( 2, \) then \( \textit{G} \) is commutative.
If \( \textit{G} \) is commutative, then a subgroup of \( \textit{G} \) need not be commutative.
correct_answer: If for all \( x,y \in \textit{G}, \; (xy)^{2} = x^{2} y^{2}, \) then \( \textit{G} \) is commutative.
If for all \( x \in \textit{G}, \; x^{2} = 1, \) then \( \textit{G} \) is commutative. Here, \( 1 \) is the identity element of \( \textit{G}. \)
If the order of \( \textit{G} \) is \( 2, \) then \( \textit{G} \) is commutative.

questions.question:
Let \( X \) be a set and \( 2^{X} \) denote the powerset of \( X \).

Define a binary operation \( \Delta \) on \( 2^{X} \) as follows:
\[
A \Delta B=(A-B) \cup(B-A) \text {. }
\]
Let \( H=\left(2^{X}, \Delta\right) \). Which of the following statements about \( H \) is/are correct?



GATE CSE 2023 | Question: 41
options: Every element in \( H \) has an inverse, but \( H \) is NOT a group.
For every \( A \in 2^{X}, \) the inverse of \( A \) is the complement of \( A \).
For every \( A \in 2^{X}, \) the inverse of \( A \) is \( A \).
correct_answer: For every \( A \in 2^{X}, \) the inverse of \( A \) is \( A \).

questions.question:
Consider the operators \( \diamond \) and \( \square \) defined by \( a \diamond b=a+2 b, a \square b=a b \), for positive integers. Which of the following statements is/are TRUE?



GATE CSE 2024 | Set 1 | Question: 42
options: Operator \( \diamond \) over the operator \( \square \) obeys the distributive law
Operator \( \square \) over the operator \( \diamond \) obeys the distributive law
correct_answer: Operator \( \square \) over the operator \( \diamond \) obeys the distributive law

questions.question:
Let \( Z_{n} \) be the group of integers \( \{0,1,2, \ldots, n-1\} \) with addition modulo \( n \) as the group operation. The number of elements in the group \( Z_{2} \times Z_{3} \times Z_{4} \) that are their own inverses is ___________.


GATE CSE 2024 | Set 2 | Question: 53
